title: This Free Tool Does Everything Claude Cowork Does And Runs Locally #ai #artificialintelligence

eval.summary: Promo clip for 'Robote,' a local-run personal AI OS that ingests email and meeting notes, builds a knowledge graph, and runs background agents. Claims to be a free alternative to Claude's native tooling. Supports Ollama, Claude, GPT, and other LLMs, plus MCP integrations (Slack, GitHub, Linear), voice, and web search. Content is a 60-second promotional short with no technical depth, no repo link shown, no architecture detail, and a 'DM me for the link' CTA — a classic lead-gen format. The tool's core value prop (email ingestion → knowledge graph → agent prep) directly overlaps with Alan OS + VIE pipeline already in flight. Multi-LLM support is a hard stack violation (Anthropic-only policy). Local-only architecture conflicts with P6 and P1 in a cloud-hybrid stack. No evidence of documented API, export path, or structured run records (P1, P10 fails). Zero token-efficiency evidence (P3 fail). Even if the tool is legitimate, it would require a full BUILD_OR_EXTEND audit against alan_os_server.py, VIE, and nlm_feed_builder.py before consideration.
eval.fit_rationale: Overlaps heavily with Alan OS and VIE but violates Anthropic-only LLM policy, has no documented API or export path, and is presented in a zero-substance lead-gen short with no verifiable architecture.

stack_evaluation.replaces_or_complements: Partially overlaps Alan OS (email ingestion, agent orchestration) and VIE (knowledge graph, context retrieval) — both already in active development
stack_evaluation.reasoning: Fails P1 (no documented API or export path — gated behind a DM), fails P3 (zero token-efficiency evidence provided), and fails P7 (≥60% functional overlap with Alan OS + VIE pipeline already in flight, which would require a BUILD_OR_EXTEND.md justification that cannot pass the ≥60% overlap bar). The multi-LLM pitch (Ollama, GPT) is also a direct violation of the Anthropic-only stack constraint.
